evals.prompt: Build a ReAct agent that can search a database and send emails. The agent must remember the conversation across multiple turns.
evals.expected_output: Creates a LangGraph ReAct agent with StructuredTool definitions, a MemorySaver checkpointer, and invokes it with a thread_id config for persistent memory
expectations: Uses create_react_agent from langgraph.prebuilt
Defines tools with @tool decorator or StructuredTool.from_function with Pydantic schemas
Creates a MemorySaver checkpointer and passes it to create_react_agent
Invokes the agent with a config containing configurable.thread_id
Uses ChatAnthropic or another LangChain chat model
Uses async/await (ainvoke) for the agent call

evals.prompt: I need a RAG pipeline that retrieves documents from Pinecone and answers questions using Claude. Build it with LangGraph so I can extend it later.
evals.expected_output: Implements a two-node LangGraph StateGraph with a retrieve node (Pinecone + VoyageAI embeddings) and a generate node (Claude), connected sequentially
expectations: Defines a RAGState TypedDict with question, context (list[Document]), and answer fields
Creates separate retrieve and generate async node functions
Uses PineconeVectorStore with VoyageAIEmbeddings
Builds a StateGraph, adds both nodes, and wires edges: START -> retrieve -> generate -> END
Calls builder.compile() to produce the runnable chain
Uses ChatAnthropic and a ChatPromptTemplate for the generate step

evals.prompt: Design a multi-agent system where a supervisor routes tasks between a researcher agent and a writer agent. The supervisor should keep routing until the task is complete.
evals.expected_output: Implements a StateGraph with a supervisor node that uses conditional edges to route to researcher or writer agents, which each loop back to the supervisor
expectations: Defines a MultiAgentState TypedDict with messages and next_agent fields
Creates a supervisor async node that sets next_agent based on LLM decision
Implements route_to_agent routing function returning Literal types
Creates specialized agents with create_react_agent and their own tool sets
Uses add_conditional_edges from supervisor to researcher/writer/END
Adds edges from researcher and writer back to supervisor to enable looping
Handles FINISH signal to route to END

evals.prompt: I have a slow LangChain app making redundant LLM calls with the same prompts. How do I add Redis caching to avoid duplicate API requests?
evals.expected_output: Shows how to configure a Redis LLM cache globally using set_llm_cache so identical prompts return cached responses without hitting the API
expectations: Imports RedisCache from langchain_community.cache
Imports set_llm_cache from langchain_core.globals
Creates a redis.Redis client with a connection URL
Calls set_llm_cache(RedisCache(redis_client)) to register the cache globally
Explains that identical prompt inputs will be served from cache
Does not introduce unrelated changes to the existing code

evals.prompt: Build a multi-step text processing workflow with LangGraph that: (1) extracts entities from input text, (2) analyzes those entities, and (3) generates a final summary. Use conditional routing between steps.
evals.expected_output: Implements a StateGraph with three nodes and conditional edges that route based on a current_step field in the state
expectations: Defines a WorkflowState TypedDict with text, entities, analysis, summary, and current_step fields
Implements extract_entities, analyze_entities, and generate_summary as separate async node functions
Each node updates current_step to indicate the next step
Implements a routing function with Literal return type matching node names
Uses add_conditional_edges with a routing dict mapping to node names and END
Calls builder.compile() to create the workflow
Uses async LLM calls (ainvoke) inside each node

evals.prompt: My LangGraph agent needs to persist memory in production across server restarts. We use PostgreSQL. How do I set this up?
evals.expected_output: Shows how to replace MemorySaver with PostgresSaver using a connection string, so agent state checkpoints survive restarts
expectations: Imports PostgresSaver from langgraph.checkpoint.postgres
Uses PostgresSaver.from_conn_string with a postgresql:// connection string
Passes the checkpointer to create_react_agent
Explains that thread_id scopes conversation history
Mentions that MemorySaver is for development only (in-memory, lost on restart)
Does not change how the agent is invoked — same thread_id config pattern applies
